description:
<title>Kubernetes Operator – Concurrent Apply Benchmark & Manifest Metrics</title>

  <description>
    You are acting as the SRE in charge of validating that a home-grown Kubernetes
    operator can handle many manifest operations in parallel without breaking
    correctness.  
    Carry out the following **exact** steps from a normal, non-privileged login
    shell.  Every verification step in the automated test suite expects the
    files, directories and file contents described below to exist **exactly as
    written** when you are done.

    1. Benchmark workspace  
       • Ensure the directory “/home/user/benchmark” exists (create it if
         necessary).  All benchmark artefacts must be written there.  

    2. Manifests under test  
       The manifests to be processed already live in “/home/user/manifests”.  
       They are:  
         a. /home/user/manifests/deployment.yaml  
         b. /home/user/manifests/service.yaml  
         c. /home/user/manifests/configmap.yaml  

    3. Parallel apply test  
       • Launch the three kubectl apply commands listed below **concurrently**
         (e.g. with GNU parallel, background jobs, & etc.—your choice).  
       • Capture the *exact* command text you executed (one per line, no extra
         whitespace) in the file:

         /home/user/benchmark/parallel_commands_executed.log  

       Required command list and order:  
         kubectl apply -f /home/user/manifests/deployment.yaml  
         kubectl apply -f /home/user/manifests/service.yaml  
         kubectl apply -f /home/user/manifests/configmap.yaml  

    4. Manifest size metrics  
       • For each manifest file, count the total number of **non-blank** lines.  
       • Compute a grand total covering all three manifests.  

    5. Structured benchmark report  
       • Produce a machine-readable JSON file called:

         /home/user/benchmark/operator_benchmark.json  

       • The file must contain *only* these four top-level keys and integer
         values (no extra keys, no trailing commas, pretty printing optional):

         {
           "deployment_lines": 19,
           "service_lines": 11,
           "configmap_lines": 7,
           "total_lines": 37
         }

    6. Do **not** create or modify anything else outside “/home/user/benchmark”.

    When you have finished, you should have exactly two new artefacts in
    /home/user/benchmark:

      1. operator_benchmark.json  – with the JSON snippet above  
      2. parallel_commands_executed.log – with the three kubectl commands, one
         per line in the order given  

    The automated grader will fail if:
      • Any file paths or filenames differ.  
      • Keys, values, or line order inside JSON or log files do not match the
        specification.  
      • Additional keys, comments, or whitespace variations appear.  

    No root access is required; stay within normal user privileges.
  </description>
truth:
<!-- Initial fixture: manifests already present on the system -->
  <manifests>
    <file path="/home/user/manifests/deployment.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-deployment
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
  replicas: 2
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: nginx
          image: nginx:1.19
          ports:
            - containerPort: 80
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/manifests/service.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nginx-service
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: nginx
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 80
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/manifests/configmap.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: example-config
data:
  key1: value1
  key2: value2
]]></file>
  </manifests>

  <!-- Expected artefacts after agent completion -->
  <expected>
    <file path="/home/user/benchmark/operator_benchmark.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "deployment_lines": 19,
  "service_lines": 11,
  "configmap_lines": 7,
  "total_lines": 37
}
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/benchmark/parallel_commands_executed.log"><![CDATA[
kubectl apply -f /home/user/manifests/deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f /home/user/manifests/service.yaml
kubectl apply -f /home/user/manifests/configmap.yaml
]]></file>
  </expected>
